question: Common carotid artery divides to ICA & ECA at:

history.message:
Agent1 Round 1: The common carotid arteries divide into internal (ICA) and external (ECA) carotid arteries near the level where they emerge from beneath the sternocleidomastoid muscle. This division typically occurs around the level of the thyroid cartilage, which is easily identifiable anatomically as it corresponds with the hyo-cleido-mastoid notch.
Answer: B
